title: sideritis glacialis subsp. pulvinata
intro: Sideritis glacialis subsp. pulvinata, also known as white mountain tea, is a subspecies of the Sideritis glacialis plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of white mountain tea are attributed to its ability to improve digestion, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of white mountain tea typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed on its own or combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
